one_line_thesis: Heritage Global is a small multi-segment asset-services croupier (auctions, lab resale, NLEX charged-off brokerage) with specialty lending / JV assets and a new DebtX platform, trading below book pending earnings-power confirmation.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Heritage Global is a multi-segment asset-services croupier with NLEX as the largest fee line, plus auctions, lab resale, specialty lending, and a new DebtX platform acquired in January 2026. FY2025 was profitable; Q1 2026 profit dipped. At about $1.26 per share, Marvin's proof-first component base case implies 8.7% per year over five years to $1.91 per share economic value, a watch stance below our mid-teens hurdle. Dhando is partial: tangible net assets and cash provide a floor, but borrower concentration and DebtX integration are real risks. No approved third-party sources are in the base...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

components.evidence: FACT FY2025 10-K segment op. income: Auction/Liquidation $2.671M, Refurbishment & Resale $1.659M, Brokerage (NLEX) $6.116M; corporate ($4.965M). CALC fee segments after corporate = $5.481M; after ~25% tax proxy ≈ $4.11M. JUDGMENT low/base/high ≈ 8×/10×/12× that after-tax run-rate ($0.95/$1.18/$1.42 before rounding). Q1 2026 still profitable but softer (NI $0.717M). Sources: 10-K FY2025 segment note; 10-Q Q1 2026.
components.scenario_assumptions: Low/base/high change earnings power and exit multiple; not a single terminal multiple on revenue.
components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in evidence.
components.falsifier: Through-cycle after-tax fee earnings fall such that capitalized value is below the low case.

Commercial Loans segment operating loss ($606) thousand Q1 2026

components.evidence: FACT: Jan 1, 2026 acquired substantially all assets of The Debt Exchange Inc. for ~$8.5M (10-Q). Q1 Commercial Loans segment op. loss ($0.606M). Goodwill rose to $12.747M; intangibles to $6.066M. JUDGMENT: only incremental value beyond fee-franchise / tangible assets already counted; failure value zero if platform does not scale.
components.scenario_assumptions: Low failure; base modest earn-in; high assumes platform approaches NLEX-like contribution over time.

probability_and_timing.timing_basis: The estimate is assessed over the five-year valuation horizon following the January 1, 2026 acquisition, rather than as immediate owner cash.
probability_and_timing.probability_basis: The low/base/high range explicitly assigns failure, modest earn-in, and a NLEX-like contribution path; Q1 2026 Commercial Loans operating loss supports a conservative base.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: No separate owner-funded expansion capital is modeled; the $8.5M acquisition consideration is already paid and the incremental option value is after the current operating loss and asset base.

components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in evidence.
components.falsifier: DebtX remains loss-making and impaired below the low case (zero).

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: Component changes are classified from observable differences in quantity, comparable method, probability, timing, capital, evidence, and schedule scope.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Auction, ALT, and NLEX fee franchises (after corporate overhead)
valuation_proof.evidence: FACT FY2025 10-K segment op. income: Auction/Liquidation $2.671M, Refurbishment & Resale $1.659M, Brokerage (NLEX) $6.116M; corporate ($4.965M). CALC fee segments after corporate = $5.481M; after ~25% tax proxy ≈ $4.11M. JUDGMENT low/base/high ≈ 8×/10×/12× that after-tax run-rate ($0.95/$1.18/$1.42 before rounding). Q1 2026 still profitable but softer (NI $0.717M). Sources: 10-K FY2025 segment note; 10-Q Q1 2026.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in evidence.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Through-cycle after-tax fee earnings fall such that capitalized value is below the low case.

valuation_proof.evidence: FACT Q1 2026: current notes receivable net $4.369M + non-current $4.182M = $8.551M ($0.25/sh). Specialty Lending FY2025 op. income only $0.230M. JUDGMENT haircut carrying for credit/collection risk.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in evidence.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence supports economics below the low case or reveals an omitted claim.

valuation_proof.evidence: FACT Q1 2026 carrying value $19.442M ($0.56/sh). FY2025 equity-method earnings only $0.123M (vs $2.688M in 2024). JUDGMENT: base discounts carrying for illiquidity and lumpy realizations; high near carrying after recent DLZ exit evidence.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in evidence.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence supports economics below the low case or reveals an omitted claim.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Cash, inventory, PP&E net of operating working liabilities

JUDGMENT haircut for inventory realization and PP&E specialized-use risk.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in evidence.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence supports economics below the low case or reveals an omitted claim.

valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in evidence.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence supports economics below the low case or reveals an omitted claim.

timeline.label: Revenue moved -79.3% versus the prior period (26,159.0 to 5,416.0).
Operating income moved -37.0% versus the prior period (9,066.0 to 5,711.0).
Closed acquisition of The Debt Exchange (DebtX) commercial-loan brokerage platform for ~$8.5M, adding Commercial Loans segment.
FY2025 net income $3.6M on $5.7M operating income; NLEX brokerage remained largest fee segment at $6.1M segment operating income.
Largest specialty-lending borrower defaulted on required minimum payments; 76% of gross notes receivable concentration disclosed.
KNFH II LLC joint venture entered Fenton, Missouri pharmaceutical plant purchase-and-sale (equity-method investment path).
Heritage Global Capital (HGC) launched specialty lending to charged-off / nonperforming asset investors.
National Loan Exchange (NLEX) charged-off receivables brokerage integrated as core fee franchise.

industry.structure: Fragmented asset-services and financial-asset brokerage: industrial auctions, lab-equipment resale, charged-off loan brokerage, and specialty lending compete with larger appraisers, auction houses, and debt buyers.
industry.share_shift: HGBL gains share when distressed asset volumes rise and when it partners in joint ventures; NLEX scale is a relative strength versus smaller regional brokers.
industry.trend: Higher charged-off consumer and commercial loan supply supports NLEX and DebtX; specialty-lending concentration and borrower default are headwinds; insider buying in 2026 is modest context only.

top_claims.claim: Heritage Global is a multi-segment asset-services croupier with NLEX as the largest fee line, plus auctions, lab resale, specialty lending, and a new DebtX platform acquired in January 2026. FY2025 was profitable; Q1 2026 profit dipped. At about $1.26 per share, Marvin's proof-first component base case implies 8.7% per
*Primary risk: Specialty-lending borrower default and concentration (76% of gross notes from one borrower, FY2025 10-K) impair notes receivable and segment income.
DebtX integration: Q1 2026 Commercial Loans operating loss and $5.3M goodwill require earn-in proof (10-Q).

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Specialty-lending borrower default and concentration (76% of gross notes from one borrower, FY2025 10-K) impair notes receivable and segment income.
DebtX integration: Q1 2026 Commercial Loans operating loss and $5.3M goodwill require earn-in proof (10-Q).
Fee cyclicality: Auction and NLEX volumes fall in benign credit cycles (10-K MD&A).
